title: What Will Win Best Adapted Screenplay? 2026 Oscars Preview, Predictions
text: The category features a dominant frontrunner with these odds: One Battle After Another at -3500, Hamnet at +1000, Train Dreams at +1600, Frankenstein at +2800, and Bugonia at +2800. One Battle After Another is Paul Thomas Anderson's adaptation of Thomas Pynchon's Vineland, which successfully modernizes a 1990 novel about Reagan-era revolutionaries. The screenplay demonstrates strong Academy appeal and represents Anderson's likely path to Oscar recognition. Hamnet is co-written by director Zhao and novelist Maggie O'Farrell, telling a heartbreaking story of love and loss within a Shakespearean setting. Observers suggest this represents the primary upset threat in the category. Train Dreams is a Netflix period piece adapted from Denis Johnson's 2011 novel, offering modest upset potential as a character-driven narrative. The article's analysis strongly favors One Battle After Another, describing it as demonstrating clear screenplay craftsmanship that aligns with voter preferences. Any alternative outcome would constitute a significant surprise in the best adapted screenplay race.
